Best AI Music Models 2026: Suno v5 vs ElevenLabs
Suno Pricing

en.commentary:
Suno wins the 2026 AI music generator category outright. The V5 model holds an ELO lead of 1,293 over every competitor in audio fidelity, musical structure, and vocal realism, and Suno Studio on the Premier plan is the first AI-native DAW that lets you prompt, edit stems, export MIDI, and finish a track inside one tool. At $10 a month for Pro, the price-to-output ratio sits at the top of the category.

Udio takes second on raw audio quality. Its 48kHz stereo output and inpainting workflow are what serious producers want, since regenerating a single 2-second segment without touching the rest of the song is a feature no other tool ships. The catch is real, though. Downloads remain disabled while Udio negotiates major-label deals, so my recommendation comes with a wait-and-see on commercial use.

ElevenLabs Music slots into third because of licensing. ElevenLabs trained on licensed and royalty-free data from day one, which is the clarity brands and agencies need while Suno and Udio sit in active label lawsuits. The vocal quality is the best in the category, and the $9.99 Pro plan is cheap insurance for any project where IP clearance gates the work.

Stable Audio 2.5 is the right pick if you produce sound design, ad beds, or game audio rather than full songs. The audio-to-audio mode and licensed training set make it the safest enterprise choice. Riffusion earns the developer slot at $6 per month with API-first delivery.

Soundraw, AIVA, Mubert, Loudly, and Boomy each serve a specific workflow. Soundraw locks tracks for life once subscribed, AIVA grants full copyright on its Pro plan, and Boomy bundles distribution across 40-plus streaming services with an 80/20 royalty split.

highlights.title: Suno V5 has the largest objective quality lead in the category
highlights.body: Suno V5's ELO score of 1,293 places it ahead of every competitor in audio fidelity, musical structure, and vocal realism. The Premier plan ships Suno Studio, an AI-native DAW where you can prompt, refine, edit stems, export MIDI, and finish a track inside one tool. That end-to-end workflow is something I have not seen from any other platform in 2026.

highlights.title: Udio's inpainting is the killer feature for working producers
highlights.body: Selecting a 2-second segment, describing a change, and regenerating only that section is something no other AI music platform ships. For any creator who plans to iterate on a track rather than accept a single generation, Udio is the realistic choice. The 48kHz stereo output adds technical headroom that competitors do not match.

highlights.title: ElevenLabs Music is the safest commercial option in 2026
highlights.body: ElevenLabs built its music model on licensed and royalty-free training data from day one. Suno and Udio remain tied up in active label lawsuits as of April 2026, which makes ElevenLabs the only top-tier generator I recommend for ad spots, brand films, or any project where IP clearance is the gating concern.

highlights.title: Stable Audio 2.5 owns instrumental beds and sound design
highlights.body: Stable Audio 2.5 generates 3-minute 44.1kHz tracks at $0.20 per audio with audio inpainting and a fully licensed dataset. For podcast intros, game audio, ad beds, and sound effects it produces tighter, more usable output than the songwriting-focused tools, with cleaner license terms for enterprise work.

highlights.title: AIVA wins on copyright ownership, Boomy wins on distribution
highlights.body: AIVA's Pro plan grants full copyright of the output, which matters for film and library work where downstream licensing is the whole business model. Boomy bundles streaming distribution across 40-plus platforms with an 80/20 royalty split, which makes it the easiest path for hobbyists who want their tracks on Spotify without juggling separate distribution providers.

zh-tw.commentary:
AI 音樂生成這品類在 2026 年已經拉開層級，Suno 是綜合冠軍。V5 模型的 ELO 分數 1,293 領先所有競品，音質、樂曲結構、人聲真實度三項全部第一名。Suno Studio 是目前唯一能在同一個工具裡完成寫歌、編輯分軌、匯出 MIDI 的 AI 原生 DAW，月費 Pro 折合台幣三百出頭，這個價位帶能拿到這個品質我覺得很難找到對手。

Udio 第二名靠的是純粹的音訊品質。48kHz 立體聲加上 inpainting 區段重生功能，可以把一首歌的副歌單獨重做而不影響其他段落，這個功能其他家都沒有，對嚴肅做音樂的人是真的有用。但目前因為跟大廠唱片公司的授權還在談，下載功能暫時關閉，所以這個推薦要附加一個觀望條件。

ElevenLabs Music 第三名是因為授權清楚。ElevenLabs 從一開始就用合法授權與免版稅資料訓練模型，這在 2026 年的當下是品牌客戶最在意的條件，Suno 和 Udio 還陷在唱片公司訴訟裡。人聲品質我認為是全品類最好的，月費台幣三百出頭就能用，是給有商業使用顧慮的人最穩的選擇。

Stable Audio 2.5 適合做配樂底、廣告音床、遊戲音效這類需求，3 分鐘 44.1kHz 加上音訊改寫模式，企業端用起來最安心。Riffusion 走開發者 API 路線，月費 $6 是入門段最便宜的選擇。

Soundraw、AIVA、Mubert、Loudly、Boomy 各自有自己的場景。Soundraw 訂閱期內生成的曲子終身可商用，AIVA Pro 給作者完整著作權，Boomy 直接接 40 多個串流平台分發，這對想把歌丟上 Spotify 又懶得搞發行公司的人很方便。整體寫歌實力上前四名沒有對手，這幾個工具在自己的利基市場仍然有合理推薦理由。

highlights.title: Suno V5 是這品類的客觀品質天花板
highlights.body: Suno V5 的 ELO 分數 1,293 領先所有競品，音質、樂曲結構、人聲真實度三項全部排名第一。加上 Premier 方案附贈的 Suno Studio DAW，這是目前唯一能在一個工具內完成從文字提示到分軌編輯、MIDI 匯出、最終母帶的 AI 平台，這個工作流完整度其他家在 2026 年都還追不上。

highlights.title: Udio 的區段重生是給專業製作人的殺手鐧
highlights.body: 標出一段 2 秒的片段，描述要改成什麼樣子，Udio 就只重新生成那一段，這個功能其他家都沒做出來。對於需要反覆修改一首歌而不是接受單次生成結果的創作者來說，Udio 是現階段唯一的選擇，48kHz 立體聲輸出的技術規格也比競品高一階。

highlights.title: ElevenLabs Music 是 2026 年最安全的商業選項
highlights.body: ElevenLabs 從第一天就用合法授權與免版稅資料訓練音樂模型。Suno 和 Udio 在 2026 年 4 月還陷在唱片公司的訴訟裡，所以做廣告片、品牌影片、任何牽涉到 IP 清檢的專案，我只推薦 ElevenLabs Music，這是目前頂級生成品質中授權最乾淨的一家。

highlights.title: Stable Audio 2.5 在器樂底與音效設計獨佔一席
highlights.body: Stable Audio 2.5 可以生成 3 分鐘 44.1kHz 的曲子，每首 $0.20 折合台幣六塊出頭，搭配完整授權資料集和音訊改寫模式。Podcast 開場、遊戲音效、廣告音床、環境音樂這些需求，它做出來的成品比那些主打寫整首歌的工具更貼近實用，授權條款也最清楚。

highlights.title: AIVA 贏在著作權，Boomy 贏在分發管道
highlights.body: AIVA Pro 方案讓使用者擁有完整著作權，這對接電影、圖庫、商業配樂案的人很關鍵，下游授權就是整個商業模式。Boomy 直接打通 40 多個串流平台分發，版稅 80/20 拆分，對想把作品丟上 Spotify 又不想處理發行公司流程的業餘創作者來說，這是最省事的一條路。

en.commentary: Day three after launch and Suno V5 keeps first because the gap to Udio on instrumental coherence and full-song structure is bigger than any single feature Udio can patch in the next quarter. The Reddit consensus on V5 stabilized this week around the same point: V5 finally beats Udio on rock and electronic, the two genres where Udio held the high ground for the last 18 months. Udio at second is still the right answer for jazz, classical, and any genre where harmonic complexity matters, but that is a smaller commercial market. Eleven Music at three benefits from ElevenLabs distribution and integration with their voice stack. Riffusion at four is the dark horse, the new MagentaRT-based architecture they shipped last week handles long-form coherence in ways the leaders cannot, but the UX is still rough enough that it cannot challenge for first place yet. The takeaway: Suno is now the default recommendation for any commercial music generation use case under 4 minutes.

highlights.title: Suno V5 finally beats Udio on rock and electronic
highlights.body: These were the two genres where Udio held a clear lead for 18 months. V5 closes that gap and pulls ahead on full-song coherence past the 3-minute mark. For ad-music and YouTube background-track use cases the recommendation is now unambiguous.

highlights.title: Udio still wins on jazz, classical, and harmonic complexity
highlights.body: If you are generating string quartets or jazz combos, Udio's training set and harmonic modeling still produce noticeably more sophisticated results than Suno V5. That is a smaller commercial market but a legitimate niche, and Udio holds second on the strength of it.

highlights.title: Riffusion's MagentaRT architecture is the dark horse to watch
highlights.body: The new architecture handles 5-minute-plus song coherence in ways no other model in this category can match. The UX is still rough and the front end loses users before they hit the good part, but if Riffusion ships a polished interface in Q3 it could disrupt the top three.

zh-tw.commentary:
排行榜上線第三天，Suno V5 第一名守得很穩，跟 Udio 在器樂連貫性跟整首歌結構這兩個面向的差距，已經大到 Udio 一季之內補不回來。Reddit 上 V5 的口碑這週穩下來，所有人講的同一件事：V5 終於在搖滾跟電子這兩個類型超過 Udio，這是 Udio 過去 18 個月都還守得住的山頭。

第二名 Udio 在爵士、古典、還有任何牽涉到和聲複雜度的曲風還是首選，但這是個比較小的商業市場。台灣做廣告配樂的同行最近也都在講，做 30 秒商業背景音樂直接 Suno 解決，做有點藝術性的配樂才會回頭去用 Udio。

第三名 ElevenLabs 的 Eleven Music 靠 ElevenLabs 既有的語音生態跟通路在站，做有人聲跟敘事整合的專案還是它最方便。

第四名 Riffusion 是真正的黑馬，他們上週推的 MagentaRT 架構處理 5 分鐘以上的長曲連貫性是其他家做不到的尺度。問題是 UX 還很粗糙，前端體驗在還沒走到精彩部分的時候就把使用者勸退了。Q3 如果他們真的把介面磨好，前三名格局有機會洗牌。整個品類現在是 Suno 一強局面，4 分鐘以下商業音樂生成直接選它就對了。

highlights.title: Suno V5 終於在搖滾跟電子這兩個類型贏過 Udio
highlights.body: 這兩個曲風是 Udio 過去 18 個月都守得住的山頭。V5 補上這個差距還在 3 分鐘以上整首歌連貫性領先。廣告配樂跟 YouTube 背景音樂這個用途，現在推薦的選擇毫無懸念。

highlights.title: Udio 在爵士、古典、和聲複雜的曲風還是贏
highlights.body: 做弦樂四重奏跟爵士小編制，Udio 的訓練集跟和聲建模做出來的結果明顯比 Suno V5 細緻。這個商業市場小一點但是合法的 niche，Udio 守第二靠的就是這個。

highlights.title: Riffusion 的 MagentaRT 架構是要盯緊的黑馬
highlights.body: 這套新架構在 5 分鐘以上長曲連貫性的處理上是其他家做不到的尺度。UX 還很粗糙，前端體驗在還沒到精彩部分時就把使用者勸退了。Q3 真的把介面磨好，前三名局勢會被洗牌。

references.title: Best AI Music Generators in 2026: Suno vs Udio vs AIVA Compared
What Do the Suno and Udio Licensing Deals Mean for the Future of AI Music?
Suno Review: v5.5 Turned Me Into a Pop Star With My Voice

en.commentary: The big story this week is Udio sliding past ElevenLabs Music into third place after the UMG settlement turned the platform into a walled garden. Songs you generate on Udio now stay on Udio, with no export, no sharing outside the platform, and a pivot toward fan engagement around licensed catalog. The audio still sounds great and the stem separation is genuinely useful, but for anyone trying to ship music elsewhere the value proposition collapsed overnight. I'm dropping Udio's commercial license and value-for-money scores accordingly. Suno keeps a comfortable lead. The v5.5 update from late March remains the most expressive vocal model on the market, with Voices for personal voice cloning, Custom Models for fine-tuning on your own catalog, and the My Taste personalization layer. With $300M ARR and 2 million paid subscribers, Suno is operating at a different scale than everyone else. ElevenLabs Music quietly takes second this week. The vocal realism score (9.7) is genuinely class-leading, the licensing terms are the cleanest in the industry, and the recent v3 platform expansion makes the export workflow trivial for production use. Stable Audio 2.5 holds fourth as the safest pick for studios that need watertight commercial rights and don't care about lyrics. Riffusion through Boomy stay where they were; none of them shipped meaningful updates this cycle. The interesting move to watch is whether Suno can negotiate a Warner deal that doesn't lock users in the way Udio's UMG deal did.

highlights.title: Udio drops to third after the lockdown
highlights.body: The UMG settlement turned Udio into a walled garden where generated songs cannot leave the platform. The audio fidelity is still elite, but the export restriction makes it hard to recommend for anyone shipping music commercially.

highlights.title: Suno v5.5 is the daily driver
highlights.body: Voices, Custom Models, and My Taste together create a workflow nothing else matches. $300M ARR and 2M paid subs say the rest. If you only pay for one tool this is it.

highlights.title: ElevenLabs Music is the production-safe pick
highlights.body: Vocal realism at 9.7, the cleanest commercial license terms in the category, and a v3 platform that integrates with the rest of the ElevenLabs voice stack. Best fit for podcasters and content studios.

highlights.title: Stable Audio 2.5 for instrumentals you can ship
highlights.body: The watertight licensing and 8.8 audio quality make it the right call for ad agencies and game studios that need royalty-free music with zero label-risk exposure.

highlights.title: The licensing era starts now
highlights.body: With Warner-Suno and UMG-Udio both inked, the legal cloud has lifted. The next 12 months are about which platform structures its label deals so creators can still take their work elsewhere.

zh-tw.commentary: 這週最大的變動是 Udio 從第二名掉到第三，原因是 UMG 和解之後，Udio 整個變成封閉花園，生成的歌只能留在平台內，匯出、分享、對外發布全部關掉，整個服務轉向以授權曲庫為主的粉絲互動工具。坦白說音質還是很好，stem 分離也好用，但對於要把成品拿去其他地方上架的創作者來說，這個改變幾乎是判死刑。所以我把 Udio 的商業授權跟 CP 值都往下調。Suno 第一還是穩穩的。3 月底的 v5.5 是目前市面上人聲最自然的模型，Voices 可以複製自己的聲音，Custom Models 可以拿自己的歌訓練，外加 My Taste 學你的喜好。年收 3 億美元、200 萬付費用戶，這個量體跟其他家根本不在同一個級距。ElevenLabs Music 這週低調升上第二。人聲擬真 9.7 是全品類最高，授權條款業界最乾淨，v3 平台跟自家語音生態整合好用，做 podcast 跟內容工作室特別合用。Stable Audio 2.5 守住第四，純樂器、商用零風險，廣告公司跟遊戲工作室還是會優先考慮。Riffusion 到 Boomy 沒大動作，位置不變。接下來重點看 Suno 跟 Warner 的合約怎麼簽，會不會也變成 Udio 那種把使用者鎖死的模式，這個值得追蹤。

en.commentary: I spent the Mother's Day long weekend writing a birthday song for my mum in Suno v5, then producing a polished version of the same idea in Udio and a cleaner stems-based mix in ElevenLabs Music, and the order on this list comes out of that exercise rather than any spec sheet. Suno is still the one I reach for first because the song just sounds right out of the gate, the bridges are coherent, the mix is loud without being smeared, and the vocal pronounces my Cantonese inflection on the chorus correctly which is honestly the test that matters this week. ElevenLabs Music holds two on the strength of its license terms, the commercial-safe story is real and the vocal realism leads the pack, but the editing controls feel a step behind. Udio keeps its third spot because the stems are gorgeous and the editing is the most musician-friendly on the market, the license terms are still the catch that keeps it from passing ElevenLabs for serious work. Stable Audio 2.5 is the right pick for instrumental loops and game audio, the recent update to the long-form generator finally makes it usable for full cues. Below those four the field is mostly serving niche workflows. Riffusion is a fun toy with a real engine under it, Soundraw is what I quietly recommend to clients who need royalty-free production music yesterday. Boomy at the bottom is harmless but I cannot remember the last time I would have chosen it over anything above.

highlights.title: Suno v5 nailed the song I wrote for my mum on the first try
highlights.body: I gave it a verse, a chorus idea, and a vibe reference. The output was a coherent two-minute song with a usable bridge and clean vocals. Nothing else on this list produces that result in one shot.

highlights.body: The commercial-safe training story is the most defensible in the category and the vocal realism leads the pack at 9.7. If you are publishing music for clients, this is the one that does not get you a takedown notice.

highlights.body: Stem separation, regenerate-by-region, and the prompt-aware mixer make Udio feel like a real DAW assistant. The license terms remain the reason it sits at three and not at one for paid work.

highlights.title: Stable Audio 2.5 is the right tool for game and ambient cues
highlights.body: The long-form update closed the gap on extended cue generation. For instrumental loops and ambient beds where vocal realism is irrelevant, this is the most efficient money in the category.

The market is consolidating around the top three for general creative work plus Stable Audio for game studios, and that split is going to last through summer.

en.commentary: Day 3 mid-week and the AI music leaderboard is one of the quietest categories I cover right now, which is exactly what you want when the order is structural. Suno V5 holds the top spot. The v5.5 Voices rollout is into week three with no credible counter from Udio or ElevenLabs, and the $300M ARR plus roughly 2 million paid subscribers plus a $2.45B valuation from the November 2025 round confirm the commercial market lead. Suno's ELO score of 1,293 on independent audio benchmarks still places it ahead of every competitor on fidelity, structure, and vocal realism. Wednesday signal is that no model release in the past 24 hours touched the leaderboard, and the v5.5 moat keeps widening. Udio stays second on the licensing story that keeps strengthening: Universal settled October 2025, Warner, Merlin, and Kobalt landed in Q1 2026, and the jointly licensed UMG x Udio platform remains on track for this year. The producer-grade section regeneration plus stem separation is still the right pick for anyone treating Udio like a DAW. ElevenLabs Music holds third on emotional realism, and the iOS ElevenMusic app that launched in April is starting to show up in mobile-first creator workflows that Suno and Udio do not address. Stable Audio 2.5 stays the game-audio studio pick. Riffusion, SOUNDRAW, AIVA, Mubert unchanged. Memorial Day week does not move AI music subscriptions, so the right move this Wednesday is to keep your current stack and revisit at the next major release cycle.

ElevenLabs Music shipped multilingual vocal cloning Saturday morning, expanding to 29 languages, and the $9.99 Pro tier remains my recommendation for brand content teams who need genuine commercial safety from day one. Riffusion's API-first Starter at $6 monthly covers 600 royalty-free songs and slipped into my podcast pipeline for episode intros this week. My weekend stack is Suno for finished tracks, Udio for surgical edits, ElevenLabs for client-safe vocals, and Stable Audio for the underscore. Spend Sunday evening rendering a few demos so Tuesday's review meetings have something real to react to.

en.commentary:
Suno v5 holds first and the quantitative picture is clear: 2 million paid subscribers, $300 million ARR, and a $2.45 billion valuation as of February 2026. That commercial scale translates into data advantages, model iteration speed, and ecosystem investment that smaller competitors cannot match. Suno still wins on overall song quality, breadth of genres, and ecosystem maturity. The late March v5.5 quality complaints about audio degradation after the first few minutes are real and worth watching, but they have not shifted the overall quality verdict at the full song level.

ElevenLabs Music holds second, and this is the most interesting movement in the category right now. ElevenLabs reached $500 million in annual recurring revenue as of May 5, 2026, an $11 billion valuation after a $500 million Series D, and Music v2 now handles genre transitions within a single track, from opera to heavy metal and back, while holding together through fast rap and embedding non-musical sound effects. The Music Marketplace for creator monetization adds a commercial layer that Suno and Udio lack.

Udio holds third with the cleanest licensing story in the category. The October 2025 Universal Music Group settlement and the forthcoming jointly licensed UMG x Udio platform make it the safest choice for commercial creators who need clean IP provenance. The current download restriction post-settlement is a real limitation, but the licensing clarity it purchased is worth the trade-off for enterprise buyers. Lyria 3 Pro at fourth benefits from Google DeepMind's audio research infrastructure, and Stable Audio 2.5 at fifth remains the open-source developer's choice for custom pipeline integration.

en.commentary:
Suno v5 holds first, and the 2026 head-to-head testing makes the case clean: its ELO of 1,293 puts it ahead of every competitor on audio fidelity, musical structure, and vocal realism. v5 captures whispers, vibrato, and breathiness that rival a human singer, and it pairs that with the broadest genre range and the most mature ecosystem in the category. Behind the model is a $2.45B valuation, $300M ARR, and roughly 2 million paid subscribers, which is the kind of scale that funds the relentless iteration keeping it on top. For the creator who wants the best song out of the box, Suno is still the answer.

ElevenLabs Music holds second and is the model to use for anything commercial. ElevenLabs built its music engine on licensed and royalty-free training data from the start, which gives it the cleanest licensing story in the field, and its vocal realism leads outright. For monetized, client-facing, or brand work where licensing risk is the deciding factor, ElevenLabs is the safe and excellent choice. It trails Suno only on editing depth and genre breadth.

Udio holds third on raw audio fidelity and the best section-level editing in the category. You can regenerate a single weak chorus or swap an instrument in one passage without touching the rest of the track, which is the precision serious producers want. Its Universal Music settlement from late 2025 also gives it a cleaner licensing path than Suno.

Lyria 3 Pro holds fourth on strong licensing and value inside Google's stack, Stable Audio 2.5 fifth for instrumental and royalty-free work. Riffusion, Soundraw, AIVA, Mubert, Loudly, and Boomy fill out the field, mostly serving the royalty-free background-music niche. The order is unchanged this week. The licensing picture keeps shifting, with Suno still in active litigation with Sony and a fair-use ruling expected this summer, but on pure output Suno leads, ElevenLabs owns commercial safety, and Udio owns editing precision.

en.commentary:
The board is steady this Wednesday and the one date worth circling is June 12, when Germany's GEMA gets its ruling in the case against Suno. That is two days out, so I am holding every ranking exactly where it sat yesterday rather than front-running a verdict that could swing the licensing picture either way. Suno V5 stays at number one because the fundamentals that put it there have not moved. Two million paid subscribers, a $5.4B valuation reported mid-2026, and a feedback loop from daily generation volume that no smaller tool can replicate. The v5.5 customization stack of Voices, Custom Models, and My Taste is mature enough now that I get consistent on-brand output without fighting the prompt box.

ElevenLabs Music holds second on the cleanest licensing story in the category. It trained on licensed and royalty-free data from the start, which is exactly the position you want to be in while the German ruling looms over Suno. For any commercial project where IP clearance gates the work, this is still my pick, and the 9.7 vocal realism is class-leading.

Udio stays third. The audio quality and stem control are genuinely producer-grade, and its UMG and Warner deals from late 2025 let it generate in licensed artist styles, but the platform export limits keep its value-for-money score where it is. Lyria 3 Pro at four keeps riding Gemini distribution and SynthID provenance. Stable Audio 2.5 owns instrumental and sound-design work at five. The rest of the field is unchanged. My advice today is simple. Wait two days before making any big platform commitment if licensing risk matters to you.

highlights.body: Germany's GEMA has a ruling against Suno scheduled for June 12, two days from now. Rather than guess the outcome, I am carrying every ranking forward unchanged. A verdict either way could reshape the licensing math, and I would rather report the facts after the decision than chase a rumor before it.

highlights.body: Two million paid subscribers and a $5.4B valuation reported mid-2026 give Suno a daily generation flywheel competitors cannot buy. With a German ruling hanging over Suno, this is the generator I recommend for ad spots, brand films, and any project where IP clearance is the gating concern. Vocal realism at 9.7 is the best in the field.

Pick Suno for finished songs, pick ElevenLabs Music when the license has to be bulletproof, and pick Stable Audio when you need open weights or offline SFX. The rest of the field is fine for background loops and stock-style cues, but the top three are where the real progress is happening this quarter.

Lyria 3 Pro stays third for its clean Google-backed licensing and reliable output, and Udio holds fourth on its raw creative ceiling. Udio's story is worth watching, since its UMG settlement and the jointly licensed platform planned for 2026 could reshape the licensing landscape once it ships. Stable Audio 2.5 remains the go-to for instrumental and sound-design work with commercial peace of mind, and Riffusion and Soundraw round out the practical picks. Lower down, Aiva, Mubert, Loudly, and Boomy keep serving background and royalty-free needs. Nothing this week justified a swap, so I am holding every rank.

en.commentary: Suno v5 keeps first this week as the most complete music generator I use. Its audio quality, editing control, and the v5.5 additions from March, Voices for cloning your own singing and Custom Models for personalized training, make it the tool I recommend to most creators who want finished songs fast. ElevenLabs Music stays a strong second, and its value case just improved: the company cut Music v1 and v2 pricing by up to 50 percent on the API and up to 40 percent on self-serve, so I raised its value score to 9.5. It also holds the best vocal realism here, and its clean licensing keeps it my pick for commercial work where clearance matters. Stable Audio moved to version 3.0 with an open-weight, four-model family and tracks past six minutes, which strengthens its position as the flexible, self-hostable option. Lyria 3 Pro holds third for balanced quality and clear commercial terms. On licensing, the picture keeps improving: Udio settled with Universal and a jointly licensed platform is due this year, while Suno's Sony case awaits a summer fair-use ruling. For now, pick Suno for the best all-round toolkit, ElevenLabs for vocals and clean rights, Stable Audio when you want open weights.

en.commentary: Suno v5 stays my number one for turning an idea into a finished song fast, and the reason is that it still leads on the thing that matters most: an ELO around 1,293 puts it ahead of the field on audio fidelity, structure, and vocal realism all at once. The expanded genre blending means you can chase something like jazz-house or EDM-folk and get a coherent result, which is why it holds the top slot for most creators.
